title: West Coast Deals
meta_desc: Explore the best family deals in West Coast, New Zealand, including current booking discounts, activity savings, and money-saving tips for a budget of $150-$250/day for a family of 4.
intro: The West Coast of New Zealand is a stunning destination for families, with its dramatic landscapes, wild beaches, and rich history. With a budget of $150-$250/day for a family of 4, you can experience the best of this region without breaking the bank. From free activities like visiting the Punakaiki Pancake Rocks to discounted campervan hires, we've got you covered.

sections.heading: Current Booking Deals and Discounts
sections.body:
Currently, Bookme Activity Discounts is offering up to 40% off activities in various locations, including Rotorua, Queenstown, Bay of Islands, and Auckland. Discover Cars is also providing family campervans from $89/day, allowing you to compare prices across different brands like Jucy, Maui, Britz, and Mighty in one search. Additionally, Booking.com is offering free cancellation on most holiday park and motel bookings.

For travel insurance, Cover-More Family Travel Insurance provides comprehensive coverage from $4.50/day. Our main booking affiliate, Bookme.co.nz, offers activity discounts of up to 40% off, making it an excellent resource for families planning their West Coast trip.
list: Up to 40% off activities with Bookme Activity Discounts
Family campervans from $89/day with Discover Cars
Free cancellation on most bookings with Booking.com
Comprehensive travel insurance from $4.50/day with Cover-More

sections.heading: Best-Value Activities
sections.body:
The West Coast offers a range of free and low-cost activities that are perfect for families. The Punakaiki Pancake Rocks and Blowholes are a must-visit, and the Franz Josef Glacier and Fox Glacier are accessible via short walks from the car park. The Hokitika Gorge walkway is another stunning free activity, featuring a turquoise river that's perfect for a family picnic.

For a budget of $150-$250/day, consider visiting the Hokitika town for greenstone carving and trying some wild foods at a local cafe. The Lake Ianthe is also a hidden gem, offering quiet, calm, and warm water during mid-summer.
list: Franz Josef Glacier and Fox Glacier (free short walks)
Hokitika town (greenstone carving and wild foods cafe)
Lake Ianthe (quiet, calm, and warm water during mid-summer)

sections.heading: Money-Saving Tips for Families
sections.body:
To make the most of your West Coast trip, consider the following money-saving tips: book your activities and accommodations in advance to secure the best prices, pack your own food and snacks to avoid eating out, and take advantage of free activities like visiting the Punakaiki Pancake Rocks and Hokitika Gorge walkway.

Additionally, look for package deals that include campervan hire, accommodation, and activities, as these can often work out cheaper than booking each component separately. Be sure to also compare prices across different brands and websites to find the best value for your budget.
list: Book activities and accommodations in advance
Pack your own food and snacks
Compare prices across different brands and websites
Consider visiting during the off-season for lower prices

sections.heading: When to Book for Best Prices
sections.body:
To get the best prices for your West Coast trip, it's essential to book your activities, accommodations, and campervan hire in advance. Consider booking during the off-season, from April to October, when prices tend to be lower. Additionally, be flexible with your travel dates and look for last-minute deals or special promotions.

By planning ahead and being mindful of your budget, you can have a fantastic family holiday on the West Coast without breaking the bank. With its stunning landscapes, rich history, and range of free and low-cost activities, this region is an ideal destination for families looking for an unforgettable adventure.
